rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers referenced in assumptions and guarantees are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex when defining AGREE assumptions and guarantees.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE expressions resolve to valid AADL features or declared constants before applying repairs.
Ensure that range assumptions and guarantees are placed in annex agree only when concrete bounds or named constants are provided.
When fixing AGREE diagnostics, trace unresolved identifiers to their root cause and correct the reference or remove the invalid clause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions with known types; avoid using undefined or untyped identifiers.
Component type contracts should not include subcomponent ports unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE identifier errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use appropriate scope for each contract element.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
